title: Problems with \n or \r
author: Mafi78
body:
Hi,
I am using this tool for an addon to export the results to excel - when i assign those values in the like thisworksheet.getCell ("C2").value = "My Text with \na line break" on multiple cells i get a strange behaviour. In Chrome this works in some cases (in others not).
In IE this is not working at all. 

What is needed to be able to debug it ?
Is there a method to export an worksheet Object in Javascript ?
Thank you for your excellent work !
I am using the latest source (5 days ago ...)
Best regards
Manfred

comments.author: Rycochet
comments.body: Should have been fixed about a month ago (see #296) - are you using the 0.4.13+? (Be careful if using from JSDelivr as it seems to have a problem with keeping up to date lately...)

comments.author: jimaek
comments.body:
This is the new endpoint that points directly to npm http://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/exceljs@0.5.0/dist/exceljs.min.js

Its automatically updated and works great

comments.author: Mafi78
comments.body:
i've seen the entry that it should have been fixed - therefore i am wondering what i did wrong !
I used the npm way ?? is there a problem ? i will try the exceljs.min.js link that you've send me

comments.author: Rycochet
comments.body:
@jimaek I think npmjs is the problem - it sometimes reports different versions for the listings and the actual entries - there's an open issue regarding it that will hopefully get fixed at some point :-)

@Mafi78 Second thing I'd try is adding the normal MS newline stuff, and putting "`\r\n`" instead (I think that's what I do where I use this) - not sure if it's necessary at all, but worth trying ;-)

comments.author: MartinKolarik
comments.body: @Rycochet any chance you got a link to the mentioned issue?

comments.author: Mafi78
Rycochet
Mafi78
Mafi78
Rycochet
Mafi78
Mafi78
comments.body:
the flag for the multiline is this setting:
worksheet.getCell("A1").alignment = { wrapText: true };

then the content is also shown on multiple lines. If this is not set the cell has the height of 1 line and you can see the second (or third) line when you manually click in the cell and hit enter
But this does not help in my IE problem :(
Do you have another idea ?

comments.author: Mafi78
comments.body: i tested it with firefox - and there are also problems with assigning the \n character ??

comments.author: Rycochet
comments.body:
Not sure about Firefox, but you could add ES6 shims for IE - https://github.com/paulmillr/es6-shim - Object.assign also copies over (like `jQuery.extend()`), and it also needs some string ones. That repo has standalone ones too if you want to look at them instead of a bigger (but easier) "everything" fix for IE.

edit: Another (possibly better) polyfill - https://github.com/zloirock/core-js

comments.author: Mafi78
comments.body:
ES6-Shim was a very good tip - now i can use exceljs.js without modifications !
I found an example and will try to upload it (webcontent.zip)
It includes a requirejs base with an error1.html that behaves different on chrome and IE and Firefox.
With chrome i can see always newlines and in IE and firefox for the cells C1 to H1 there is no newline ?!
I used the following value for this 'My text with two \n\n newlines' if i change it to 'My text with two \n \n newlines' it works - also in IE. 

Due to the fact that my application is embedded in an application it is hard for me to reproduce the error. Is it possible to save the workbook object with javascript (stringify it JSON.stringify) and read this text on my other application with JSON.Parse ?

Then i would make sure it has something todo with the other application !
Thanks

comments.author: Mafi78
comments.body: [WebContent.zip](https://github.com/guyonroche/exceljs/files/1171100/WebContent.zip)

comments.author: Rycochet
comments.body: A general search shows a lot of people complaining about inconsistent newlines, but nothing definitive on what any actual problem is - hopefully someone else has more time to look into it :-/

comments.author: IRCraziestTaxi
comments.body: I would love an option to use windows carriage returns instead of the standard \n. The app I wrote to transform data and output to CSV generates the files with \n newlines and I have to open in excel and save to get windows newlines because the ingestor that reads the files just has to have windows newlines, apparently. So it's not a huge deal - exceljs already did 99% of the work :) - but it would be nice to have it as an option in CsvWriteOptions.

comments.author: trigg-mosaic
comments.body:
For some reason I still have this issue with 4.1.1 (unless I'm just doing something else wrong):

None of the fixes here or in #296  worked for me. What finally worked was to create a string with backticks and then hit the enter key between them, store as a const, and then add to whatever string where I needed a break.

```
const lineBreak = `
`;

const myText = `This is my text.${lineBreak}`;
```

I don't know enough to know if there's a better way, but it worked.

comments.author: JeanBaptisteBolh
comments.body: Hi all, I am having issues with this still (almost 2025).  I've tried everything in this thread but no dice.  Let me know if anyone comes up with anything.
